noveltyJustification: 2 new local findings proved the checked-in Copilot path is notification-only, and 1 authoritative GitHub Docs finding showed the upstream hook API can still inject additionalContext, which turns X3 into a transport split instead of a platform dead end.

focus: X4 Codex PreToolUse/PostToolUse payload + blocking semantics
noveltyJustification: Upstream Codex docs plus runtime/parser code closed X4 by showing PreToolUse can deny only current Bash invocations, PostToolUse cannot undo side effects, and additive concurrent hook execution pushes idempotency and dedupe into packet-owned policy design.

focus: X5 adversarial advisor / prompt injection surface
noveltyJustification: Local code inspection closed the main split: raw prompt text can bias routing because explicit skill mentions are boosted directly, but the checked-in repo still has no brief renderer that reflects arbitrary prompt text back to the model. The new evidence also pinned phase 019/003's sanitizer as the right trust-boundary carry-over and downgraded timing attacks to bounded latency shaping rather than persistent poisoning.

This pass turned wave-1 observability placeholders into an implementation-ready contract by binding them to checked-in health, hook, and telemetry surfaces: advisor health_check fields, runtime cache diagnostics, benchmark/regression gates, and the existing session_health section model. The result closes X6 with a concrete metric namespace, JSONL invocation log schema, alarm thresholds, and a peer advisor-hook-health section instead of a vague "add telemetry later" note.

This pass closed the migration gap between live SKILL discovery and process-cached graph/brief reuse. The new evidence showed discovery already invalidates on (path, mtime_ns, size), inventory parity already reports discovery-vs-graph drift, and renames are true identity changes, which made it possible to specify an exact per-skill fingerprint contract and a deleted-skill fail-open/suppress path instead of vague cache-TTL language.

This pass separated true torn-read risks from stale-snapshot risks. The checked-in SQLite skill-graph path already gives committed-snapshot reads via WAL + a single transaction, but the repo still has two concurrency gaps: JSON fallback writes are non-atomic, and long-lived process caches plus unlocked read-merge-write hook-state updates can diverge across concurrent Claude sessions.

focus: X9 NFKC sanitization interaction for advisor brief rendering
noveltyJustification: This pass closed the renderer-boundary question by separating typed advisor fields from free-text fields. The new evidence showed the future brief can stay small and safe if it whitelists freshness/confidence/uncertainty, canonical-folds only the visible skill label, and suppresses the brief whenever the normalized label becomes instruction-shaped; the heavier recovered-payload provenance gate is unnecessary for this one-line surface.

This pass did not discover a new runtime surface, but it converted nine wave-2 investigations into one converged execution contract, confirmed the child-spec sequence still holds, and reduced the remaining work to validation-only checks.
